subject: SIP Weekly Update - Week ending 31 Oct

content.answer:
Hi Ian,

Pls find SIP weekly update attached for your review.

Thank you.
Cuiwei

content.question:
Thanks.



Slide 13

   - For new direct lanes, how does the SKU efficiency compare with SIP?

- For import to SG, @Jason Yu <jason.yu@shopee.com> will be the PIC
   right?
Can remind me where we land on MY?

Slide 17

   - For Ads TR here, what is the denominator?
Total direct GMV ?

Seems
   very high?
- How about ADO coverage?
Why so high for VN?

Slide 18

   - Do not understand this very well. How does CN sellers use paid ads

How does it compare to what SEA Direct Sellers have?

   to have the same access?

content.answer:
rebate 50% of ads top up through platform sponsored vouchers; 3) provide
   adxpress service
   -

   Data validated with both SGBI and CB FP&A


   -

   How about ADO coverage? Why so high for VN?
   -

      ADO coverage = lane level Ads ADO / total ADO
      -

      Higher ADO coverage for VNMY direct at 46% due to lower ads CPO at
      $1.1 vs all other lanes (e.g., IDMY $4.6). VNMY lower ads CPO
driven up by
      top 10 shops 100% ads adoption with low ads CPO; IDMY out of 10
top shops,
      6 without ads adoption.
      -

      Next step: working with ID team to drive up top sellers ads investment

 [image: image.png]



Slide 18

   -

   Do not understand this very well. How does CN sellers use paid ads right
   now? How does it compare to what SEA Direct Sellers have?

[image: image.png]

   -

   CNCB sellers primarily use overseas shop balance to purchase ads, i.e.,
   deduct the cost from their shop earnings. Under existing Shopee system
   design, this payment method is only possible when seller’s overseas shop is
   linked to a 3PP wallet (e.g. Payoneer).
   -

   For SEA Direct sellers as-is
   -

      MY export sellers are able to purchase ads the same way as CNCB
      sellers, as they are using 3PP wallet (Payoneer), continuing since MYSG
      Direct launch last Mar
      -

      For ID/VN export sellers at launch, aligned with Christin/Anh to 1)
      launch without 3PP to improve seller adoption; 2) improve system
to enable
      shop balance purchase without needing 3PP wallet by EOY; 3)
interim to use
      offline manual adjustment for seller payout - which cannot be
used for ads
      purchase due to system limitations


   -

   And from a product point of view, what are we building to allow them to
   have the same access?
   -

      Product is developing the system feature to enable SEA Direct sellers
      to use overseas shop balance to purchase ads credit v

Hi Ian,


Slide 13

   -

   For new direct lanes, how does the SKU efficiency compare with SIP?
   -

      Overall still lower than SIP, except for MYSG

[image: image.png]

   -

   Reason 1: Newly onboarded SKUs start with low SKU eff diluted overall
   SKU effi. Compare SKU effi across Jul to Sep onboarding months across 3 top
   lanes as below, SKU effi gap with SIP narrowed as SKUs matured; MYSG
   Jul-Aug batches, VNMY Jul batch already have SKU effi better than SIP

content.question:
§  Root cause: seller’s price is still high as only 50% of ID sellers used
the best local price to do the overseas pricing





Why is this the case?

*From:* Cuiwei Sun <cuiwei.sun@shopee.com>
*Sent:* Wednesday, 5 November 2025 10:16 pm
*To:* Ian Ho <hoi@sea.com>
*Cc:* Jason Yu <jason.yu@shopee.com>; Jianghong Liu <
jianghong.liu@shopee.com>; Shuning Wang <shuning.wang@shopee.com>; Keljin
Chua <keljin.chua@shopee.com>; Yu Zhang <yu.zhang@shopee.com>; Ailin Xie <
ailin.xie@shopee.com>; Wei Qiang <wei.qiang@shopee.com>; Huang Wan-Jung <
wanjung.huangwj@shopee.com>; Chiong Zhi Ann <zhiann.chiong@shopee.com>; Wei
Loong Yong <weiloong.yong@shopee.com>; Linda Sun <linda.sun@shopee.com>;
Xiao Xuan Soh <xiaoxuan.soh@shopee.com>; Keith Huang <keith.huang@shopee.com>;
Joe Li <joe.liyc@shopee.com>; Lim Wan Ting <wanting.lim@shopee.com>; Ren
Jiahui <jiahui.ren@shopee.com>; Eveline Seah <eveline.seahyl@shopee.com>;
Lee Jia Yi <jiayi.jylee@shopee.com>; Faye Yan <faye.yan@shopee.com>
*Subject:* Re: SIP Weekly Update - Week ending 31 Oct



Hi Ian,



Slide 13

   - For new direct lanes, how does the SKU efficiency compare with SIP?

- Overall still lower than SIP, except for MYSG

o   Reason 1: Newly onboarded SKUs start with low SKU eff diluted overall
SKU effi. Compare SKU effi across Jul to Sep onboarding months across 3 top
lanes as below, SKU effi gap with SIP narrowed as SKUs matured; MYSG
Jul-Aug batches, VNMY Jul batch already have SKU effi better than SIP



o   Reason 2: Lower traffic exposure

§  Root cause: sellers face pain-points in ads top up and estimating ads
budget

§  Next steps: 1) short-term: Reg Direct team to explore co-fund ads
investment with import markets; ETA end-Nov; 2) long term: System feature
in development to allow seller purchase with shop balance within seller
center, ETA Dec-Mar

o   Reason 3: Lower CR, especially in ID export lanes

§  Root cause: seller’s price is still high as only 50% of ID sellers used
the best local price to do the overseas pricing

§  Next steps: In progress of working with ID team to negotiate price with
top 20 sellers, ETA mid-Nov

o   Reg team will focus on ads investment and price comp improvement as P1
in Nov, then move to additional reg initiative push in Dec, including

§  Seller investment: AMS, video, livestream

§  Conversion improvement: CS, key ops metrics



   - For import to SG, @Jason Yu will be the PIC right?

Can remind me where
   we land on MY?

- Yes aligned that @Jason Yu <jason.yu@shopee.com> team will own the
      Direct selling ADO into MY and into SG, this will include


   - topline/PnL targets
         - marketing program/resources
         - Seller/SKU acquisition direction in export market
         - Optimization across import BUs to drive growth


   - Regional direct team will focus on cross-lane efforts including


   - Launch new lanes, secure local resources to start business
         - Align and report regional topline target, track progress across
         lanes, coordinate market resources across lanes
         - Initiate and drive regional initiatives to improve seller
         experience and drive growth across lanes (ads investment, price comp,
         content enablement)
         - Establish and operate inter-market infrastructure and process
         (system, logistics, payment, CS, ops policy)



Slide 17

   - For Ads TR here, what is the denominator?
Total direct GMV ?

Seems
   very high?

o   Denominator = total direct GMV at lane level

o   Oct Ads TR across lane <5%, all below import market CNCB average, and
most lower than their local shop (except MYSG), indicating room to improve

o   MYSG Direct TR higher than seller’s local shop because SG platform has
provided incentives to Direct sellers including 1) 20% off paid-ads top-up;
2) rebate 50% of ads top up through platform sponsored vouchers; 3) provide
adxpress service

o   Data validated with both SGBI and CB FP&A

   - How about ADO coverage?
Why so high for VN?

- ADO coverage = lane level Ads ADO / total ADO
      - Higher ADO coverage for VNMY direct at 46% due to lower ads CPO at
      $1.1 vs all other lanes (e.g., IDMY $4.6). VNMY lower ads CPO
driven up by
      top 10 shops 100% ads adoption with low ads CPO; IDMY out of 10
top shops,
      6 without ads adoption.
      - Next step: working with ID team to drive up top sellers ads
      investment







Slide 18

   - Do not understand this very well. How does CN sellers use paid ads
   right now?
How does it compare to what SEA Direct Sellers have?

o   CNCB sellers primarily use overseas shop balance to purchase ads, i.e.,
deduct the cost from their shop earnings. Under existing Shopee system
design, this payment method is only possible when seller’s overseas shop is
linked to a 3PP wallet (e.g. Payoneer).

o   For SEA Direct sellers as-is

§  MY export sellers are able to purchase ads the same way as CNCB sellers,
as they are using 3PP wallet (Payoneer), continuing since MYSG Direct
launch last Mar

§  For ID/VN export sellers at launch, aligned with Christin/Anh to 1)
launch without 3PP to improve seller adoption; 2) improve system to enable
shop balance purchase without needing 3PP wallet by EOY; 3) interim to use
offline manual adjustment for seller payout - which cannot be used for ads
purchase due to system limitations

   - And from a product point of view, what are we building to allow them
   to have the same access?

- Product is developing the system feature to enable SEA Direct sellers
      to use overseas shop balance to purchase ads credit via seller center.
      Target to roll-out in mid Dec after 12.12. Timeline for each
export market
      as below:


   - ID / VN Export - by Dec 2025
         - MY / SG Export - by Jan 2026
         - Others - by Mar 2026

Slide 21

   - What is this about?
